writing.task1: I write to connect about the non-compete clause in my offer. The wording feels unclear and might block me from freelance work not related to my role. For example, helping a friend with a simple website could be misunderstood. Could we revise or add a note to say the clause only applies to direct competitor. That would make things clearer for both sides
writing.task2: I agree that every student should learn a basic “AI literacy” class. Today AI is everywhere, so knowing how it works is important. For example, a business student should understand how AI tools change jobs and markets. But as an artist, I cannot accept AI in art - it cannot replace human feeling or creativity. Some people say adding this class makes study too heavy. Yet if the course is short and simple, it will not be a big burden. Learning about AI does not mean we must use it; it helps us see its limits and protect human work, especially in art
writing.task3: The trial showed clear contrasts: Rubberized Asphalt cut 2.4 dB and complaints 9% but was very costly at $220k/km; Delivery Window Shift was cheapest ($40k/corridor) yet only reduced 1.1 dB and 6% complaint; Facade Baffles gave the best effect, lowering 3.2 dB and 12% complaint for $160k/block. Given this balance, scaling Facade Baffles is most cost-effective, with Delivery Shifts as a cheap add on and Asphalt only where resurfacing is planned

testData.testTitle: IELTS Starter 3.0 Practice Test (EN–VI) — Grammar & Meaning Focus
